title: Anthropic x-api-key survives cross-host provider redirects
source: An operator-selected or compromised Anthropic endpoint controls 3xx Location responses after receiving a keyed request.
closest_control: The shared reqwest client uses its default ten-hop redirect policy; pinned reqwest 0.12.28 strips Authorization/Cookie classes on cross-host redirects but not the custom x-api-key header.
sink_or_broken_control: A 307/308 redirect replays the request to another host with x-api-key and the request body intact.
impact: The configured Anthropic credential and governed prompt can be disclosed to an attacker-controlled redirect destination.
plausibility: Static trace confirms custom header insertion, default redirects, and the dependency's exact cross-host removal list, which omits x-api-key.

validation.evidence: reqwest-0.12.28 src/redirect.rs:239-249 removes AUTHORIZATION, COOKIE, cookie2, PROXY_AUTHORIZATION, and WWW_AUTHENTICATE only; provider/anthropic.rs:85 uses x-api-key
validation.proof_gap: No live redirect server harness was run because target code is read-only.

attack_path.attacker: compromised configured Anthropic endpoint, DNS path, or operator-selected endpoint that can issue redirects
preconditions: gateway has a nonempty Anthropic API key
endpoint returns a body-preserving cross-host redirect
steps: authenticated caller selects the Anthropic-mapped model
upstream returns 307/308 Location to attacker host
attack_path.closest_control_failure: redirect policy does not constrain origin and the custom credential header is not stripped
severity_facts: valid gateway caller is needed to trigger provider use
provider compromise or destination control is needed
credential can authorize additional Anthropic spend/data access

title: Arbitrary AOG_LOCAL_BASE is treated as non-cloud egress
source: Production operator configuration controls AOG_LOCAL_BASE; the backend itself is untrusted and may also redirect.
closest_control: Cloud/local enforcement derives only from target.provider == local. Neither startup nor OpenAiProvider::local validates loopback/private address, approved origin, redirect confinement, or HTTPS.
sink_or_broken_control: Raw classified prompts are sent to the arbitrary local-base destination while policy/tokenization believes no cloud egress occurs.
impact: A production misconfiguration or redirecting local backend can exfiltrate regulated prompts off-appliance without the cloud policy block or tokenization.
plausibility: main.rs directly loads AOG_LOCAL_BASE and registers it under the security-significant local name; surface branches skip stream blocking/tokenization when target_is_cloud is false.

validation.evidence: main.rs:87-89 accepts the environment URL; policy.rs:273-274 classifies solely by name; surface stream/non-stream sends raw content for non-cloud; provider/openai.rs:76-82 performs the request
validation.proof_gap: Deployment egress ACLs were not inspected by this file worker.

attack_path.attacker: malicious operator configuration, compromised deployment configuration, or compromised local backend with redirect control
preconditions: a caller submits governed content to the default/local model
steps: endpoint is registered as provider name local
stream or non-stream path skips cloud egress protections
attack_path.closest_control_failure: semantic local label is not bound to a validated network destination
severity_facts: production does not reject the weakening
regulated payload impact can be high

title: OpenAI base URL accepts plaintext HTTP while sending the bearer key
source: AOG_OPENAI_BASE is privileged operator-controlled production configuration.
closest_control: No constructor/startup check requires https for a keyed provider.
sink_or_broken_control: Authorization: Bearer <key> is sent to the configured HTTP URL.
impact: Network observers or a malicious intermediary can steal the OpenAI key and view governed prompts when production is misconfigured to HTTP.
plausibility: The base URL is concatenated directly and reqwest permits http; rustls support does not enforce TLS use.

validation.evidence: main.rs:97-98 accepts AOG_OPENAI_BASE; provider/openai.rs:50 stores it and 79 adds Authorization before send

steps: gateway sends keyed prompt over plaintext
attack_path.closest_control_failure: key presence is checked but transport security is not

source: AOG_ANTHROPIC_BASE is privileged operator-controlled production configuration.
closest_control: No constructor/startup check requires https for a keyed provider.
sink_or_broken_control: x-api-key is sent to the configured HTTP URL.

validation.evidence: main.rs:106-107 accepts AOG_ANTHROPIC_BASE; provider/anthropic.rs:39 stores it and 85 adds x-api-key before send

steps: gateway sends keyed prompt over plaintext

title: OpenAI-compatible non-stream responses have no body-size or total-duration bound
source: The configured OpenAI-compatible backend controls status, Content-Length/transfer encoding, bytes, and timing.
closest_control: Only connect and idle-read timeouts exist; Response::text/json collect without a byte cap and a trickling body can keep resetting the idle timeout.
sink_or_broken_control: Both successful JSON and non-2xx text bodies are fully buffered/decoded.
impact: A compromised or malicious backend can exhaust gateway memory and hold request tasks/connections, degrading the appliance for all tenants.
plausibility: Direct reqwest body consumers are present with no content-length precheck, take(limit), incremental cap, or total deadline.

validation.evidence: provider/openai.rs:87 calls text and 156-159 calls json; provider.rs intentionally omits total timeout
validation.proof_gap: No large-body runtime harness was run.

preconditions: an authenticated request reaches that provider
steps: return very large or endless trickled 2xx/non-2xx body
idle timeout never fires while bytes arrive
attack_path.closest_control_failure: no response byte cap or total deadline
severity_facts: one backend can affect multi-tenant gateway availability

sink_or_broken_control: Both successful JSON and non-2xx text bodies are fully buffered/decoded.

title: OpenAI streaming SSE permits unbounded newline-free buffering and duration
source: The OpenAI-compatible streaming backend controls chunk boundaries, newline placement, bytes, and stream duration.
closest_control: A 120-second idle read timeout fires only when no bytes arrive; no per-line, aggregate, frame-count, or total-duration limit exists.
sink_or_broken_control: sse_stream appends lossy-decoded chunks to one String until a newline appears.
impact: A backend can trickle an arbitrarily long line and grow gateway memory indefinitely while occupying a stream task.
plausibility: The loop and buffer are directly visible and have no length check.

validation.evidence: provider.rs:200-226 pushes every chunk and drains only after buf.find newline; provider/openai.rs:182 delegates directly
validation.proof_gap: No adversarial SSE server harness was run.

steps: backend sends bytes more frequently than 120 seconds without newline
no parse or terminal condition is reached
attack_path.closest_control_failure: idle timeout is not a size or total-time bound
severity_facts: client disconnect may eventually drop one stream, but concurrent triggers amplify impact

title: OpenAI non-stream metering accepts missing or falsified provider usage
source: The untrusted OpenAI-compatible backend controls the usage object and its numeric values.
closest_control: Missing/null usage returns None and the completion substitutes Usage::default; invalid fields individually become zero. The surfaces record and spend exactly those values.
sink_or_broken_control: A successful completion with absent/zero/underreported usage is receipted and charged as zero or too little.
impact: A compromised or nonconforming backend can defeat budget enforcement, cost accounting, and audit evidence while still returning model output.
plausibility: The default-to-zero parse and direct settlement chain are explicit.

validation.evidence: provider/openai.rs:171 uses unwrap_or_default; surface_openai.rs:244 and 253-259 settle from r.usage without fallback
validation.proof_gap: Requires a provider that omits or falsifies usage; no live provider harness was run.

attack_path.attacker: compromised/malicious OpenAI-compatible backend or operator-selected nonconforming backend
preconditions: provider returns 2xx completion with bad usage
steps: receipt and lineage spend use it
future preflight sees too little spend
attack_path.closest_control_failure: provider telemetry is treated as authoritative without validation or local lower bound

title: Anthropic non-stream metering accepts missing or falsified provider usage
source: The untrusted Anthropic backend controls usage fields and numeric values.
closest_control: Missing, oversized, nonnumeric, or underreported usage becomes zero/low values; the surface records and spends exactly those values.
sink_or_broken_control: A successful completion with absent/zero/underreported usage is receipted and charged as zero or too little.
impact: A compromised or nonconforming backend can defeat budget enforcement, cost accounting, and audit evidence while still returning model output.
plausibility: The default-to-zero parse and direct settlement chain are explicit.

validation.evidence: provider/anthropic.rs:185-188 uses u32_at defaults; surface_anthropic.rs:201 and 210-216 settle from r.usage without fallback
validation.proof_gap: Requires a provider that omits or falsifies usage; no live provider harness was run.

attack_path.attacker: compromised/malicious Anthropic backend or operator-selected nonconforming backend
preconditions: provider returns 2xx completion with bad usage
steps: receipt and lineage spend use it
future preflight sees too little spend
attack_path.closest_control_failure: provider telemetry is treated as authoritative without validation or local lower bound

title: OpenAI stream metering lets any positive provider count suppress local fallback
source: The untrusted streaming backend controls terminal usage frames.
closest_control: StreamMeter has a local estimate only when a reported field equals zero; any positive value, even 1 for a large request/output, wins without comparison to observed lower bounds.
sink_or_broken_control: Underreported positive usage is settled instead of the request/delta estimate.
impact: A backend can undercharge streamed calls and weaken budget and audit guarantees.
plausibility: parse_sse accepts provider values and StreamMeter's >0 branch unconditionally selects them.

validation.evidence: provider/openai.rs:133-145 carries usage; meter.rs:398-406 chooses any positive reported field instead of estimates
validation.proof_gap: No crafted stream harness was run.

steps: drop selects reported count over delta-derived estimate
attack_path.closest_control_failure: fallback checks only zero, not plausibility/lower bound

closest_control: StreamMeter has a local estimate only when a reported field equals zero; any positive value, even 1 for a large request/output, wins without comparison to observed lower bounds.
sink_or_broken_control: Underreported positive usage is settled instead of the request/delta estimate.

plausibility: parse_sse accepts provider values and StreamMeter's >0 branch unconditionally selects them.

validation.evidence: provider/anthropic.rs:129-144 carries split usage; meter.rs:398-406 chooses any positive reported field instead of estimates
validation.proof_gap: No crafted stream harness was run.

steps: drop selects reported count over delta-derived estimate
attack_path.closest_control_failure: fallback checks only zero, not plausibility/lower bound

title: OpenAI surface reports truncated or length-limited streams as normal stop
source: The provider controls finish_reason and may fail mid-stream.
closest_control: parse_sse observes finish_reason only as a boolean and does not preserve it; chat_sse swallows provider errors and emits finish_reason=stop and DONE even when no terminal frame arrived.
sink_or_broken_control: Clients receive a syntactically successful normal completion for max-token truncation or transport/decode failure.
impact: A downstream consumer may act on incomplete model output as if it were complete, but no privileged tool consumer of this success signal was identified in the reviewed chain.
plausibility: The semantic rewrite is deterministic in code; security impact depends on downstream use.

validation.evidence: provider/openai.rs:134-145 drops reason; surface_openai.rs:430-438 converts error/nonclosed termination to stop plus DONE
validation.proof_gap: No in-repository privileged consumer was found that turns this false-success signal into a concrete security action.

attack_path.attacker: malicious/failing provider influencing a downstream model consumer
preconditions: consumer treats finish_reason=stop/DONE as integrity evidence for acting on output
steps: provider emits partial text then error or length finish
attack_path.closest_control_failure: provider terminal status is not preserved
attack_path.impact_path: potential unsafe action on truncated output
severity_facts: requires downstream behavior outside assigned files

title: Anthropic surface reports truncated or failed streams as end_turn
source: The provider controls stop_reason and may fail mid-stream.
closest_control: parse_sse discards message_delta stop_reason; messages_sse swallows provider errors and always emits stop_reason=end_turn plus message_stop.
sink_or_broken_control: Clients receive a syntactically successful end_turn for max-token truncation or transport/decode failure.
impact: A downstream consumer may act on incomplete model output as if it were complete, but no privileged tool consumer of this success signal was identified in the reviewed chain.
plausibility: The semantic rewrite is deterministic in code; security impact depends on downstream use.

validation.evidence: provider/anthropic.rs:137-149 carries usage/done but not stop reason; surface_anthropic.rs:299-309 converts error/termination to end_turn/message_stop
validation.proof_gap: No in-repository privileged consumer was found that turns this false-success signal into a concrete security action.

attack_path.attacker: malicious/failing provider influencing a downstream model consumer
preconditions: consumer treats end_turn/message_stop as integrity evidence for acting on output
steps: provider emits partial text then error or max_tokens stop
attack_path.closest_control_failure: provider terminal status is not preserved
attack_path.impact_path: potential unsafe action on truncated output
severity_facts: requires downstream behavior outside assigned files
